spec_match.rationale: Walks the spec-defined permission pipeline end-to-end: `enumerateDevices` (empty IDs before permission) → `getUserMedia({audio: true})` → re-enumerate (IDs revealed) → `setPreferredSinkId`. That sequence is mandated by the privacy model around device IDs.

interactive.rationale: Step-by-step buttons drive each stage with 9 edge-related terms in the source and live readouts after each call — a real walk-through of the permission flow.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `Walk the full permission pipeline: enumerate devices (IDs hidden without permission) → request getUserMedia audio → re-enumerate (IDs now visible) → call setPreferredSinkId`. Page is exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: 9 edge-term hits in source — covers the empty-deviceId case, the unrevealed-pre-permission path, and the rejection branches. Best edge-case coverage in this feature folder.

references.rationale: The page has NO `see also`/`references` section. Sections list is empty. Missing the required `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5098834847072256` link for invariant #3.

design_system.rationale: 67 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add the references section with chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the `see also` section entirely. Invariant #3: every concept page must cite `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5098834847072256` so the slug-cleanup script can recover this folder from its content. Add the section with chromestatus + mediacapture-output spec links.

openQuestions.title: Cover the permission-denied branch
openQuestions.detail: If the user rejects the getUserMedia prompt, the flow stalls at step 2. Surface that explicitly: catch the NotAllowedError and explain that without mic permission, output device IDs stay opaque even though the user only wants to pick a speaker.

openQuestions.title: Show the persistent-permission caveat
openQuestions.detail: Once permission is granted in a session, subsequent reloads may or may not retain it depending on browser policy. Add a `revoke and try again` link to `chrome://settings/content/microphone` plus an in-page explanation of the persistence rules.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detect probe at the top
openQuestions.detail: The other pages in this feature folder ship a probe; this one does not. Add `typeof navigator.mediaDevices.setPreferredSinkId === 'function'` so visitors can see support at a glance.

summary: Excellent step-by-step teach of the privacy/permission pipeline around output device selection — the most pedagogically useful page in this feature. Marred only by the missing references section (chromestatus link required) and the absence of a feature-detect probe.
